description: Profiles, Streaks & Stats Overhaul - Merge settings into account, add friend profiles with stats, enrich buddy/squad detail pages, overhaul streak logic, and enhance listing cards

userStories.title: Update streak increment logic to use group workouts
userStories.description: As a user, I want my streak to increment anytime my buddy/squad and I start workouts within 20 minutes of each other, regardless of schedule.
acceptanceCriteria: A streak day is counted if all members have overlapping workouts (within 20-min window) on that calendar day, regardless of schedule
Streak calculation uses GroupWorkout documents as the source of truth instead of individual workouts
Existing streak calculation tests updated to reflect new logic
New test: streak increments on unscheduled workout days
New test: streak increments when workouts overlap within 20 min but outside schedule
All API tests pass (cd services/api && yarn test)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Refactor streak-calculator.ts to walk through GroupWorkout documents. The existing group workout detection (20-min window) already captures overlapping workouts — use those as the data source.

userStories.title: Update streak reset logic to 7-day inactivity window
userStories.description: As a user, I want my streak to reset only after a full week (7 days) of not working out together, so occasional missed days don't break momentum.
acceptanceCriteria: A streak resets to 0 only if 7 or more consecutive calendar days pass without a group workout
Gaps of 1-6 days do NOT reset the streak (the streak pauses but is not lost)
Both Burn Buddy and Burn Squad streaks follow this rule
New test: streak survives a 6-day gap
New test: streak resets on a 7-day gap
New test: streak survives multiple short gaps
Existing streak tests updated for new reset window
All API tests pass (cd services/api && yarn test)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Builds on the streak-calculator refactor from US-001. The calculator should allow gaps of up to 6 days between group workouts without resetting.

userStories.title: Merge Settings into Account page
userStories.description: As a user, I want a single Account page that includes both my profile info and settings so I don't have to navigate between two separate pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Account page (/account) includes username editing (currently on /settings)
Account page retains existing functionality: display name, sign out, onboarding toggle
Settings page (/settings) is removed; any links to it redirect to /account
NavBar updated to remove Settings link if present
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Frontend-only change. Move username editing UI from /settings to /account, delete the settings page, and update navigation.

userStories.title: Add buddy/squad stats API endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need API endpoints that return group workout stats for a given burn buddy or squad.
acceptanceCriteria: Add GroupStats type to packages/shared/src/types.ts with fields: highestStreakEver (value + date), firstGroupWorkoutDate, groupWorkoutsAllTime, groupWorkoutsThisMonth
GET /burn-buddies/:id/stats returns: highestStreakEver (value + date), firstGroupWorkoutDate, groupWorkoutsAllTime, groupWorkoutsThisMonth
GET /burn-squads/:id/stats returns the same shape of data
Only members of the buddy pair / squad can access these endpoints (return 403 otherwise)
Uses getDb() from lib/firestore.ts for all Firestore access
All API tests pass (cd services/api && yarn test)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Calculate highest streak ever by walking full group workout history using the updated streak calculator from US-001/US-002. Add new test file following the project's vi.mock + vi.hoisted + supertest pattern.

userStories.title: Add friend profile API endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an API endpoint that returns a user's profile stats so the frontend can render friend profiles.
acceptanceCriteria: Add ProfileStats type to packages/shared/src/types.ts with fields: displayName, username, highestActiveStreak (value + buddy/squad name), highestStreakEver (value + date + buddy/squad name), firstWorkoutDate, workoutsAllTime, workoutsThisMonth, buddyRelationshipStatus
GET /users/:uid/profile returns the ProfileStats data
Endpoint requires authentication (user must be logged in)
Endpoint only returns data if requesting user is a friend of the target user (return 403 otherwise)
Returns burn buddy relationship status: none, pending_sent, pending_received, or buddies
Highest active streak and highest streak ever aggregated across all target user's buddy/squad relationships (with buddy/squad name for context)
Uses getDb() from lib/firestore.ts for all Firestore access
All API tests pass (cd services/api && yarn test)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Aggregates stats across all of a user's buddy/squad relationships. May involve multiple Firestore reads — consider batching. Add new test file following the project's vi.mock + vi.hoisted + supertest pattern.

userStories.title: Add stats to burn buddy and burn squad detail pages
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see richer stats on the burn buddy and burn squad detail pages so I can track our progress together.
acceptanceCriteria: Detail pages (/burn-buddies/[id], /burn-squads/[id]) show: active streak (already done), highest streak achieved with date, first workout date, total workouts all time, total workouts this month
Stats fetched from GET /burn-buddies/:id/stats and GET /burn-squads/:id/stats
Stats displayed in a grid/card layout (e.g., 2-column grid of stat tiles)
Loading and error states handled gracefully
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-004 (stats API). Create reusable stat display components — these will be reused on the friend profile page in US-007.

userStories.title: Add friend profile page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to tap on a friend's name on the Friends page and see their profile with workout stats.
acceptanceCriteria: Friends list items on /friends are clickable and navigate to /profile/[uid]

Profile page displays: username, display name, highest active streak (with buddy/squad name), highest streak ever (with date and buddy/squad name), first workout date, total workouts all time, total workouts this month
Request to be Burn Buddy button visible near top-right if viewer is NOT already a buddy
Button hidden when already buddies or when a pending request exists
Loading and error states handled gracefully
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-005 (profile API). Reuse stat display components from US-006. The Request to be Burn Buddy button uses buddyRelationshipStatus from the profile API.

userStories.title: Enhance burn buddies and squads listing cards
userStories.description: As a user, I want the buddy/squad listing cards to show streaks with a fire emoji and the next planned workout so the cards are more engaging.
acceptanceCriteria: Each card on Burn Buddies and Burn Squads list pages shows streak as fire emoji followed by count (e.g., 🔥12)
Fire emoji rendered inline as text, not as an image
If a next planned workout exists (from WorkoutSchedule), show next occurrence (e.g., Next: Mon 7:00 AM)
If no upcoming planned workout, the field is hidden entirely (no placeholder text)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Next planned workout derived from WorkoutSchedule on the buddy/squad. Calculate next occurrence from the schedule's day/time fields.

userStories.title: Push changes, create PR, and merge
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all changes pushed to the remote branch with a PR created and merged so the feature is delivered to main.
acceptanceCriteria: All changes committed and pushed to ralph/profiles-streaks-stats branch
Pull request created against main with a descriptive title and summary of all changes
PR description lists all user stories completed (US-001 through US-008)
All CI checks pass on the PR
PR is merged into main
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Final story. Push the branch, create a PR using gh CLI, wait for CI checks to pass, then merge. Use 'gh pr create' and 'gh pr merge' commands.
